steps.title: Recognize the no-solution condition
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has no solution when it represents parallel lines — lines with the same slope but different y-intercepts that never intersect.

steps.title: Find the slope of the given equation
steps.content:
The given equation is $7x - 27ry = c$ for some constant $c$.
To find its slope, rewrite in slope-intercept form:
$$-27ry = -7x + c$$$$y = \frac{7}{27r}x - \frac{c}{27r}$$The slope is $\frac{7}{27r}$.

steps.title: Determine the slope of the other equation
steps.content:
For the system to have no solution, the other equation must be parallel to the given equation. Parallel lines share the same slope. Therefore, the other equation has slope $\frac{7}{27r}$.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
